campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending an interim ordinance and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance number 2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue overcross planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to come back for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the full details of this project appear to be cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session discussions from late March

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several agreements and projects affecting the city's infrastructure and housing. Key decisions included approving a contract update with the airport's baggage system provider, protecting affordable housing when subsidies end, planning improvements to the storm sewer system, and moving forward with a FEMA-funded family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage handling system contract update
Protecting affordable housing when government subsidies expire
Storm sewer system citywide planning and upgrades
FEMA funding for San José Family Camp project
Approval of routine council committee minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what infrastructure projects and improvements the city wants to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Budget planning for city infrastructure projects in 2026-27
Teen Month recognition proclamation
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Capital improvement priorities for the city

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These special recognitions highlight the city's commitment to celebrating diverse communities and ensuring equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
City Council regular session
Community heritage celebration

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends taxpayer money. The meeting also covered rules about who needs to register as a lobbyist if they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and how residents can access city budget information
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
Community engagement on city spending and financial decision-making
Rules for reporting lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or challenge it in court, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file your lawsuit, unless another law gives you less time.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure about quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning documents and procedural updates. The main items included changes to how the city uses federal housing and community development funding, updates to council member work requests, and a proposed change to how city council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development funding plan for 2025-2030
Changes to how city council handles vacant council seats
Work requests submitted by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as historically important and wants permission to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the structure to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule
Downtown Commercial zone development
March 12 meeting minutes approval
